name: Todoist
description: Drive Todoist (personal/team task manager) from any AI agent: projects, tasks, comments, labels, sections. 12 tools, Bearer token auth, REST API v2.
instructions:
This connector uses the Todoist API v1 (the modern unified API — the older `/rest/v2` and `/sync/v9` are deprecated).

**Setup**:
1. Sign in to https://todoist.com → top-right avatar → **Settings → Integrations → Developer → API token → Copy**.
2. Set `TODOIST_API_TOKEN` to it.

**Authentication**: `Authorization: Bearer ${TODOIST_API_TOKEN}`. The token is per-user; everything done with it appears as that user's actions. Personal API tokens never expire.

**Hierarchy**: Project → Section (optional) → Task → Sub-task. Tasks can also live directly in a project without a section. The Inbox is a special project (`inbox_project=true`).

**Date strings**: Todoist accepts natural language for `due_string` (e.g. 'tomorrow at noon', 'every monday 9am', 'in 3 days', 'next friday') OR explicit `due_date` (YYYY-MM-DD) / `due_datetime` (ISO 8601 RFC 3339). For recurring tasks, the natural-language form is required to set recurrence rules.

**Priorities**: 1-4 in the API (1=lowest, 4=urgent). The Todoist UI shows them inverted (P1 in UI = priority 4 in API).

**Filters**: when listing tasks via `todoist_get_active_tasks`, the `filter` parameter accepts Todoist's filter syntax: 'today', 'overdue', 'p1 & @work', 'due before: +7 days', etc.

**Pagination**: not used — the REST API returns full lists (sometimes thousands of items). Use filters to narrow.

**Rate limits**: 1000 requests per 15 min per user. On 429 back off.

**Sync API not exposed here**: Todoist also has a Sync API (single batch endpoint that returns all data at once) — useful for full local mirrors but more complex. This connector uses the simpler REST endpoints.

**Out of scope here**: webhooks, attachments upload, project sharing/collaboration changes (only show collaborators), karma stats, premium-only filters management.

tools.name: todoist_get_projects
tools.description: List all projects the user can access. Each project has id, name, color, parent_id, order, comment_count, is_shared, is_favorite, is_inbox_project, url.

tools.name: todoist_create_project
tools.description: Create a new project. Required: name. Color names: berry_red, red, orange, yellow, olive_green, lime_green, green, mint_green, teal, sky_blue, light_blue, blue, grape, violet, lavender, magenta, salmon, charcoal, grey, taupe.

tools.name: todoist_get_active_tasks
tools.description: List active (non-completed) tasks with filters. The `filter` parameter is powerful — use Todoist filter syntax like 'today', 'overdue', 'p1 & @home', 'due before: +7 days'.

filter.description: Todoist filter string, e.g. 'today', 'overdue', '@work & p1', 'due before: +7 days'.

tools.name: todoist_get_task
tools.description: Fetch a single task by ID.

tools.name: todoist_create_task
tools.description: Create a task. Required: content. Use due_string for natural-language dates ('tomorrow 9am', 'every monday'). Set parent_id to create a subtask.

content.description: Task content (the title). Supports Markdown links.

project_id.description: Target project ID. Defaults to Inbox.

parent_id.description: Make this a subtask of the given task ID.

labels.description: Array of label names (auto-creates labels if new).

due_string.description: Natural language: 'tomorrow at 9am', 'every monday', 'in 3 days'. Required for recurring tasks.

due_lang.description: Language for due_string parsing (en, de, fr, ru, ja, etc.).

assignee_id.description: User ID — only valid for shared projects.

tools.name: todoist_update_task
tools.description: Update a task. To move it to a different project, use the dedicated todoist_move_task tool.

tools.name: todoist_complete_task
tools.description: Mark a task as completed. For recurring tasks, the next occurrence is auto-created.

tools.name: todoist_get_comments
tools.description: List comments on a project OR a task. Provide either project_id or task_id.

tools.name: todoist_create_comment
tools.description: Add a comment to a project or task. Required: content + one of (project_id, task_id).

tools.name: todoist_get_labels
tools.description: List the user's personal labels. Each has id, name, color, order, is_favorite.
